_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: TX state-legislature batch (Ligon SD-4, Cody Harris HD-8, Rehmet SD-9, Ashby HD-9->SD-3, Brian Harrison HD-10). Evidence-first; every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence. Roster fixes: Rehmet is a Democrat (was mislabeled R); Ashby is running for TX Senate SD-3 (won 2026-03-03 primary outright). Ligon record carries stale slug 'vacant' (real sitting SD-4 senator) - flag for rename.

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06-19: Brett Ligon (R), former Montgomery County DA, won the 2026-05-02 SD-4 special election (~75%) after Brandon Creighton resigned; sworn 2026-05-19; faces a Nov 2026 rematch vs. Ron Angeletti for the full term. Seated only weeks ago, so essentially no Senate roll-call record yet - scored from campaign positions + endorsements + 17-yr DA record. NOTE: record slug is 'vacant' (stale placeholder from the pre-special-election vacancy) and should be renamed to 'brett-ligon' in a follow-up.

vacant@TX.notes_append: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Strong documented record on public justice (career prosecutor) and life (Texas Right to Life PAC endorsement); no public positions found on marriage/LGBTQ, family/child, election integrity, christian liberty, or guns - those left null, not inferred. FOLLOW-UP: rename slug 'vacant' -> 'brett-ligon'.

0.note: Texas Right to Life PAC endorsed Ligon (2026-04-27) as a 'Pro-Life Warrior' affirming the 'God-given Right to Life from fertilization to natural death.'

1.note: No documented vote/sponsorship for abortion abolition (no Senate record yet); not inferred.

2.note: No public position found on embryonic stem-cell research / IVF embryo discard.

3.note: No explicit statement on euthanasia / assisted suicide located.

4.note: Donor history not reviewed; not scored.

0.note: Endorsed by a wall of law-enforcement groups (CLEAT, Houston Police Officers' Union, Harris County Deputies, TX Municipal Police Officers Assn); 17-yr DA brand built on public safety.

1.note: 17-year Montgomery County DA with a tough-on-crime, victims'-rights record - the opposite of a soft-on-crime / rogue prosecutor; served as special prosecutor in the capital murder of a sheriff's deputy.

2.note: DA record centered on victims' rights and proportional punishment; 100+ jury trials to verdict.

3.note: No explicit statement on treating violent crime as a public-health issue.

4.note: No explicit theological/Rom.13 statement located.

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06-19: Cody Harris (R) represents TX House District 8 since 2019; won the 2026-03-03 GOP primary vs. Daniel Hunt, faces Jeff Chavez (D) in Nov 2026. Chairs House Natural Resources. Scored from Texas Legislature roll-call record + issue-group ratings.

cody-harris@TX.notes_append: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Texas Alliance for Life 100% (2025). Documented votes: SB2 ESA/vouchers YES (2025), HB1927 constitutional carry YES (2021), anti-CBDC 'constitutional money' YES (2025), Texas Sovereignty Act YES (2025), 2021 border-security funding YES. JBS Freedom Index flags some NO votes (budget, school funding, Article V convention) but these are fiscal-hawk / con-con-skeptic positions, not documented anti-conservative votes - left unscored rather than counted FALSE.

4.note: Donor history not reviewed.

profile.confidence_note: ROSTER FIX (web-verified 2026-06-19): Taylor Rehmet is a DEMOCRAT (record previously mislabeled Republican). Air Force veteran / IAM Machinists union leader; WON the 2026-01-31 SD-9 special-election runoff vs. Republican Leigh Wambsganss (57.3%) after Republican Kelly Hancock vacated the seat; sworn 2026-02-19; advanced from the 2026-03-03 Democratic primary; faces a Wambsganss rematch in Nov 2026. Ran on economic populism and deliberately avoided culture-war issues, so most culture-war cells are null (no stated position), not false.

taylor-rehmet@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. PARTY CORRECTED R->D. Labor Democrat (IAM Machinists, AF veteran) who flipped GOP-held SD-9 in a Jan 2026 special runoff. Documented contrary position: opposes school vouchers ('ending voucher schemes'), endorsed by Texas AFT. Deliberately silent on abortion, LGBTQ, guns, immigration, and religious liberty (Texas Tribune) - those cells null, not scored. Endorsers: Texas AFL-CIO, Texas AFT, IAM, VoteVets, Texas Majority PAC. iVoterGuide logs 'Did not answer' across all policy areas. CROSS-PARTY NOTE: a Democrat who ran without using the word 'Democrat' on economic populism.

0.note: Opposes school vouchers - campaign site calls for 'fully funding public schools and ending voucher schemes that drain them' and opposes school privatization; endorsed by Texas AFT.

1.note: No position located on parental notification/consent.

2.note: No position located on CRT/SOGI/sex-ed.

3.note: No position located on porn age-verification.

4.note: No position located on faith-based adoption.

set.office: Texas State Representative, District 9 (sitting · 2026 R nominee for TX Senate District 3)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06-19: Trent Ashby (R) currently holds TX House District 9 (Lufkin); he is NOT seeking HD-9 reelection - he WON the 2026-03-03 GOP primary for the OPEN TX Senate District 3 seat outright (~62% vs. Rhonda Ward, Sen. Nichols retiring) and is the GOP nominee vs. Democrat Bobby Tillman in Nov 2026, while remaining the sitting HD-9 rep through Jan 2027. Scored on his real HD-9 roll-call record (the sitting office).

trent-ashby@TX.notes_append: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. Texas Alliance for Life 100% (2025); Texas Values Action 92% 'Faith & Family Champion' (2025); co-author of SB1 (2021 Election Integrity Act); voted YES on HB1927 constitutional carry (2021). SCHOOL-CHOICE EVOLUTION: a long-time rural voucher skeptic who voted FOR the 2023 Herrero anti-voucher amendment (one of 24 GOP defectors), then flipped in Nov 2023 and voted YES on SB2 ESA in 2025 - current position supports school choice. Freedom Index 50% (2025, declining).

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 100% (89th Lege, 2025); TAL-endorsed.

3.note: No position located on euthanasia.

4.note: Donor history not reviewed.

2.note: SB14 (2023 minor-gender-medicine ban) passed the TX House 87-56 on party lines (R for); Ashby is an R on the recorded majority - party-line marquee vote, consistent with his 92% Texas Values rating.

3.note: No position on divorce reform.

4.note: No specific LGBTQ-in-schools vote isolated (Texas Values rating is a proxy, not cell-specific).

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06-19: Brian E. Harrison (R) represents TX House District 10 (Midlothian/Ellis County) since a Feb 2021 special election; running for HD-10 reelection (NOT higher office) - won the 2026-03-03 GOP primary, faces Michael Myers (D) in Nov 2026. Texas Freedom Caucus hardliner; former acting HHS Secretary. Scored from his roll-call record + bill filings + ratings.

brian-harrison@TX.notes_append: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-19. YCT 97; co-sponsored HB41 (minor gender-medicine ban); voted NO on HB1738 (i.e., to keep the homosexual-conduct statute); led pressure on Texas A&M over gender-identity curriculum (2025); vocal SB2 ESA champion; filed HB698 (5-year property-tax abolition); voted YES on HB1056 gold/silver legal tender; voted YES on the Texas Sovereignty Act; filed HB1491 (removal of illegal border-crossers). Texas Alliance for Life 74% (mid-tier) - relative pro-life soft spot worth a future look; no FALSE scored without a documented contrary vote.
